perturbation.original_text: THIS AGREEMENT is made and entered into as of the 1st day of November, 2002, by and between TENNESSEE GAS PIPELINE COMPANY, a Delaware Corporation, hereinafter referred to as "Transporter" and LOUISVILLE GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY, a Kentucky Corporation, hereinafter referred to as "Shipper." Transporter and Shipper shall collectively be referred to herein as the "Parties."
perturbation.changed_text: THIS AGREEMENT is made and entered into as of the 1st day of November, 2002, by and between TENNESSEE GAS PIPELINE COMPANY, a Delaware Corporation, hereinafter referred to as "Transporter" and LOUISVILLE GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY, a Kentucky Corporation, hereinafter referred to as "Customer." Transporter and Customer shall collectively be referred to herein as the "Parties."
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Shipper' has been changed to 'Customer.' While both entities are involved in the agreement, using different terms creates uncertainty. Later clauses referring to Shipper are now ambiguous as to whether they apply to Louisville Gas and Electric Company.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Transportation Service - Transporter agrees to accept and receive daily on a firm basis, at the Point(s) of Receipt from Shipper or for Shipper's account such quantity of gas as Shipper makes available up to the Transportation Quantity, and to deliver to or for the account of Shipper to the Point(s) of Delivery an Equivalent Quantity of gas.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Shipper' to 'Customer' introduces a misalignment in terminology. The 'Contradicted Text' explicitly refers to 'Shipper' in the context of transportation service obligations. Because of the change in terminology, it is now unclear if the transportation service obligations apply to 'Customer' because the original text's defined term, 'Shipper', is no longer used. This creates a direct conflict regarding the applicable party in the transportation agreement as the meaning of the original intent of the contract has been altered. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 1.1 TRANSPORTATION QUANTITY - shall mean the maximum daily quantity of gas which Transporter agrees to receive and transport on a firm basis, subject to Article II herein, for the account of Shipper hereunder on each day during each year during the term hereof, which shall be 51,000 dekatherms.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 TRANSPORTATION QUANTITY - shall mean the minimum daily quantity of gas which Transporter agrees to receive and transport on a firm basis, subject to Article II herein, for the account of Shipper hereunder on each day during each year during the term hereof, which shall be 51,000 dekatherms.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of Transportation Quantity is changed from 'maximum' to 'minimum'. This introduces a contradiction as other sections may rely on the 'maximum' quantity. This conflicts Article II, about the transportation service.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Transportation Service - Transporter agrees to accept and receive daily on a firm basis, at the Point(s) of Receipt from Shipper or for Shipper's account such quantity of gas as Shipper makes available up to the Transportation Quantity, and to deliver to or for the account of Shipper to the Point(s) of Delivery an Equivalent Quantity of gas.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Transportation Quantity' from a 'maximum' to a 'minimum' daily quantity. This directly contradicts the 'Transportation Service' clause, which states the Transporter agrees to accept and receive daily such quantity of gas as Shipper makes available 'up to the Transportation Quantity'. If the Transportation Quantity is now the 'minimum', the Transporter is obligated to accept at least that amount, but the original clause implies that the Shipper may provide less, up to a maximum. The original clause allows for variation up to the maximum, while the modified definition creates an obligation to provide a minimum quantity, causing a direct conflict and potential breach if the Shipper provides less than the 'minimum' Transportation Quantity.

perturbation.original_text: Pursuant to Article VIII, Section 1 of the General Terms and Conditions of Transporter's Tariff ("GT&C"), Transporter shall cause the delivery of natural gas to Shipper at the Shipper's Primary Point of Delivery as nearly as practicable to Transporter's line pressure, provided that such line pressure shall not be less than 500 pounds per square inch gauge at Monroe, meter number 020843, and 600 pounds per square inch gauge at Calvary, meter number 020844.
perturbation.changed_text: Pursuant to Article VIII, Section 1 of the General Terms and Conditions of Transporter's Tariff ("GT&C"), Transporter shall cause the delivery of natural gas to Customer at the Shipper's Primary Point of Delivery as nearly as practicable to Transporter's line pressure, provided that such line pressure shall not be less than 500 pounds per square inch gauge at Monroe, meter number 020843, and 600 pounds per square inch gauge at Calvary, meter number 020844.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Shipper" to "Customer" creates confusion in this section, specifically about who is receiving the gas because the Shipper Primary Point of Delivery is still being referenced even thought the customer is receiving the natural gas. Also, referencing 'Customer' conflicts the meaning if Shipper is not the Customer. This introduces ambiguity that affects the point of delivery.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 Pursuant to Article VIII, Section 1 of the General Terms and Conditions of Transporter's Tariff ("GT&C"), Transporter shall cause the delivery of natural gas to Customer at the Shipper's Primary Point of Delivery as nearly as practicable to Transporter's line pressure, provided that such line pressure shall not be less than 500 pounds per square inch gauge at Monroe, meter number 020843, and 600 pounds per square inch gauge at Calvary, meter number 020844. Transporter shall be obligated to provide such minimum pressures only to the extent that capacity is reserved by Shipper and scheduled by Transporter at the Primary Delivery Point(s) described above. Such minimum pressure obligation is subject to the GT&C including, but not limited to, Article X - Excuse of Performances. In the event Transporter is unable to maintain the minimum pressure(s) described herein but Shipper is still able to take receipt of the scheduled quantity at the Primary Delivery Point(s) described above, then Shipper shall be considered unharmed by Transporter's inability to maintain such minimum pressure(s). Subject to the foregoing, any failure on Transporter's part to deliver the scheduled quantity at the Primary Delivery Point(s) 1 described above shall entitle Shipper to the limited remedy specified in Rate Schedule FT-A, Section 7 - Failure of Transporter.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Shipper" to "Customer" introduces a contradiction because the text also references "Shipper's Primary Point of Delivery." This creates ambiguity as to whether the Customer and Shipper are the same entity, and if not, who is responsible for the delivery point. The contradicted text expects clarity on this point, making the obligation unclear and potentially unenforceable. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition leading to an in-text contradiction.
